Let's talk →

About me

Marketer who codes.
Developer who converts.

I'm Celestino Trosso, a Marketer and developer based in Angola. Studying Computer Engineering and Business Management, I've spent the last two years at the intersection of craft and engineering — making things that don't just look great but also sale.

I believe the distinction between "Marekter" and "developer" is mostly artificial. The best products come from people who can hold both in mind at once: how something looks and how it works, what it communicates and how it's constructed.

Before going independent, I worked at Colegio Politecnico Privato do Lobito helping young people writing their first line of code. I grew up in Lobito, went to university in Benguela, and decided to stay. Outside of work I am entrepreneur mentoring beginners to level up their english speaking skills, I create content, and maintain an embarrassingly large reading list.

How I work

Craft over speed

I'd rather spend an extra day getting the details right than ship something I'm not proud of. That said, I believe in iterating in the open — early, imperfect, real.

Opinions, held lightly

I'll tell you what I think and why. But I change my mind when the evidence warrants it. Good collaboration requires real positions, not just validation.

Systems over solutions

I try to solve problems in ways that prevent the next ten problems from existing. A good design system is worth a hundred one-off solutions.

Writing as thinking

If I can't explain a design decision in a sentence or two, I don't understand it well enough yet. Writing forces the precision that sketches let you avoid.
